steps.action: Verify list endpoints use respondWithEnvelope format in manifest
steps.expected: list_courses, list_lectures, list_jobs all have format: respondWithEnvelope with proper envelope structure
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: list_courses (format: 'respondWithEnvelope'), list_lectures (format: 'respondWithEnvelope'), list_jobs (format: 'respondWithEnvelope') — all include envelope with success, data, meta

steps.action: Cross-reference manifest list response formats against server.go handlers
steps.expected: server.go line 844 coursesHandler, line 885 lecturesHandler, line 946 listJobsHandler all call respondWithEnvelope()
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: server.go line 844 respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'courses', courses), line 885 respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'lectures', lectures), line 946 respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'listJobs', s.jobStore.ListJobs())

steps.action: Verify manifest restartBehavior.pending says jobs are marked as failed
steps.expected: restartBehavior.pending should say 'failed', not 'restored as pending'
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: manifest jobPersistence.restartBehavior.pending = 'Jobs in pending state are marked as failed (cannot be resumed)'. Also restartBehavior.running = 'Jobs that were running are marked as failed on restart (cannot be resumed)'

steps.action: Cross-reference restart behavior against server.go loadFromDisk()
steps.expected: loadFromDisk() should mark pending/running as failed
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: server.go loadFromDisk() sets status = 'failed' when status == 'pending' || status == 'running', with error message 'job interrupted by server restart'

steps.action: Verify manifest includes idempotencyKey and INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY error code
steps.expected: idempotencyKey parameter in download_lectures tool, INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY in errorCodes
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: download_lectures tool has idempotencyKey parameter (required: false, max 256 chars). errorCodes.codes includes INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY (status 400). download_lectures errors array includes INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY

steps.action: Verify health endpoint structured response in manifest
steps.expected: health_check tool has respondWithEnvelope format with status/config/upstream/ffmpeg fields
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: health_check tool has format: respondWithEnvelope with envelope containing success, data (status, config, upstream, ffmpeg), meta

steps.action: Verify createJob/getJob use respondWithEnvelope in manifest
steps.expected: download_lectures (201), get_job responses should be wrapped
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: server.go line 942 respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusCreated, 'createJob', job), line 962 respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'getJob', job). Manifest get_job response says 'Job object (same schema as download_lectures response)' and download_lectures has duplicateResponse with format: respondWithEnvelope

codeReferences: server.go:844 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'courses', courses)
server.go:885 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'lectures', lectures)
server.go:946 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'listJobs', s.jobStore.ListJobs())
server.go:942 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusCreated, 'createJob', job)
server.go:962 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'getJob', job)
server.go:933 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusConflict, 'createJob', ...) for idempotency duplicate
server.go:640 — respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'health', ...)
server.go loadFromDisk() — status='failed' for pending/running jobs
auth.go:99 — respondWithEnvelope definition produces {success, data, meta}
auth.go:152 — respondWithSuccess definition produces {success, data, meta}
auth.go:117 — respondWithError definition produces {success: false, error: {code, message}, meta}
previousFailures: Previously failed: manifest claimed 'bare arrays not wrapped in envelope' but all handlers use respondWithEnvelope()
Previously failed: manifest claimed pending jobs 'restored as pending' but code marks them as failed
evidence.resolution: Both previous failures appear FIXED in current manifest

steps.action: Verify 'Direct JSON responses' section correctly describes envelope wrapping
steps.expected: Section should say all handlers wrap in {success, data, error, meta} envelope using respondWithEnvelope
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: Section titled 'Direct JSON responses' states: 'All handlers wrap responses in {success, data, error, meta} envelope using respondWithEnvelope'. Lists GET /courses, GET /lectures, POST /jobs, GET /jobs, GET /jobs/{id} with correct envelope format

steps.action: Verify Restart Behavior table correctly says pending → failed
steps.expected: pending should be 'Marked as failed (cannot be resumed)' not 'Restored as pending'
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: Restart Behavior table shows: pending → 'Marked as failed (cannot be resumed)', running → 'Marked as failed (cannot resume mid-download)'

steps.action: Verify health endpoint section exists with structured response
steps.expected: Dedicated Health section with structured JSON response showing status/config/upstream/ffmpeg
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: Health section at top of api-reference.md with GET /health, 200 response showing {success, data: {status, config, upstream, ffmpeg}, meta}. Includes descriptions of possible status values (ok, degraded, misconfigured, unreachable, not_found)

steps.expected: Create job section documents idempotencyKey optional field with 409 behavior
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: Create job section lists idempotencyKey (string, max 256 chars) as optional field. Documents 409 Conflict response with envelope format showing {job, duplicate: true}. Lists INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY (400) in errors. Job Persistence section notes keys survive restarts

steps.action: Cross-reference api-reference.md against server.go handler behavior
steps.expected: All documented behaviors match actual code
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: (1) courses/lectures/jobs handlers all use respondWithEnvelope — matches docs. (2) createJob uses respondWithEnvelope for 201 and 409 — matches docs. (3) deleteJob uses respondWithSuccess — matches docs. (4) loadFromDisk marks pending/running as failed — matches restart table. (5) Health handler uses respondWithEnvelope with structured data — matches docs

steps.action: Verify envelope format sections are comprehensive
steps.expected: Three response format sections: error envelope, success envelope, direct JSON responses
steps.observed: CONFIRMED: Three sections: 'JSON error envelope (respondWithError)' with {success: false, error: {code, message, details}, meta}, 'JSON success envelope (respondWithSuccess)' with {success: true, data, meta}, 'Direct JSON responses' explaining respondWithEnvelope wrapping. All match auth.go function signatures

server.go:885 — respondWithEnvelope for lectures → matches 'Direct JSON responses' section

server.go:942 — respondWithEnvelope for createJob (201) → matches docs
server.go:933 — respondWithEnvelope for idempotency 409 → matches 409 section
server.go:640 — respondWithEnvelope for health → matches Health section
server.go loadFromDisk() — pending/running → failed → matches Restart Behavior table
auth.go:99-114 — respondWithEnvelope produces {success, data, meta}
auth.go:117-149 — respondWithError produces {success: false, error: {code, message}, meta}

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both passed. VAL-DOC-001 (manifest accuracy) and VAL-DOC-002 (API reference accuracy) both correctly reflect the actual server.go code. The two previously-reported failures (bare arrays vs envelope, and pending jobs restart behavior) have both been fixed in the current documentation.
